data.title: Frontier Comm ticks higher after Calif. PUC approves Verizon deal
data.description:
Frontier Communications (FYBR) edged higher by 0.3% after the California Public Utilities Commission voted to approve its sale to Verizon (VZ).

The Calif. PUC voted 5-0 to approve the transaction  on Thursday. 

The Federal Communications Commission approved Verizon's (NYSE:VZ) $9.6 billion purchase of Frontier (NASDAQ:FYBR) in May after the telecom firm agreed to end its diversity, equity and inclusion policies.

data.title: Woodward to exit on-highway natural gas truck business in China
data.description:
• Woodward (WWD) plans to wind down its on-highway natural gas truck business in China by the end of the fiscal year to sharpen focus within its Industrial segment.
  • The decision follows years of evaluating divestment options, with no viable sale offers emerging amid challenging market conditions in China.
  • The wind-down includes closing a small manufacturing facility and reducing related sales, engineering, and support staff.
  • The China OH business has not been a material or consistent contributor to Woodward’s overall financial performance.
  • Woodward reported record sales and earnings in fiscal 2025, driven by strong results across its Industrial and Aerospace segments.

data.title: Serina Therapeutics receives NYSE notification
data.description:
• Serina Therapeutics (NYSE: SER) on Thursday received an NYSE notice that it does not meet certain continued listing requirements under Sections 1003(a)(i) and (ii).
  • The company reported stockholders’ equity of $1.6M as of Sept. 30, 2025, and has recorded losses in three of the past four fiscal years ended Dec. 31, 2024.
  • The company must file a plan by Feb. 8, 2026, detailing how it will meet NYSE listing requirements by July 9, 2027, following a notice of non-compliance.

data.title: Invesco Mortgage estimates book value increased at least 6% since Q3
data.description:
Invesco Mortgage Capital (IVR)  estimated its book value at common share was $8.94-$9.30 as of Jan. 12, 2026, up 6.3%-10.6% from its book value at Sept. 30, 2025, the company disclosed on Thursday.

At the high end of the range, the stock's value of $8.84 at the Jan. 12 market close reflected a 4.9% discount to book value. At the low end of the book value estimate, the stock traded at a 1.1% premium to book value.

It estimated its economic debt-to-equity ratio at 6.9x as of Jan. 12, up from 6.7x at Sept. 30, 2025.

The debt-to-equity ratio is estimated at 6.5x, vs. 6.7x at the end of Q3.

Invesco Mortgage (IVR) stock rose 0.2% in after-hours trading.

data.title: J.B. Hunt Transport reports mixed Q4 results as cost management mitigates 'soft demand'
data.description:
J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) reported mixed results for the fourth quarter as effective cost management—specifically expenses associated with insurance and claims, salaries, and non-owned equipment—enabled the logistics company to earn a better-than-expected profit despite a 2% drop in revenue.

Shares are down 5% in after-hours trading.

For the final quarter of 2025, J.B. Hunt (JBHT) earned a profit of $1.90 per share, up 24% from a year ago and 8 cents above expectations. The company reduced its total operating expenses as a percentage of revenue to 92.0% from 93.4% a year earlier.

At the same time, “soft demand across many of the end markets served” resulted in a 3% decline in intermodal sales, a 1% decline in integrated capacity solutions revenue, and a 10% decline in final mile services sales. Helping to offset some of these losses was a 10% gain in truckload sales and a 1% increase in revenue in its dedicated contract services segment, resulting in $3.1B in total revenue, down 2% year-over-year and $20M below expectations.

Excluding fuel surcharge revenue, total operating revenue was also down 2%, primarily driven by a 2% and 4% decline in revenue per load excluding fuel surcharge revenue in intermodal and truckload, respectively.

Operating income for the current quarter increased 19% as the prior year's quarterly operating income was negatively impacted by pre-tax charges of $16M for intangible asset impairments.

data.title: BWX Technologies rated Neutral in new coverage at BNP Paribas
data.description:
BNP Paribas on Thursday initiated coverage of BWX Technologies (BWXT) with a Neutral rating, arguing that the company’s strong defense franchise and commercial nuclear optionality are largely reflected in the current share price.

“BWXT has an attractive defense business with commercial nuclear optionality, but this looks priced in with margin upside more of a 2027+ opportunity,” analyst Matthew Akers wrote in a research note dated Jan. 14, adding that BNP Paribas sees limited near-term total shareholder return at current valuation levels.

Core naval reactor business offers long-term visibility

Akers highlighted BWXT’s core naval nuclear propulsion business, which supplies reactors for U.S. submarines and aircraft carriers, as a key strength underpinning the company’s long-term outlook.

“BWXT's core naval reactor business has strong demand well into the 2030s and beyond for the U.S. as well as likely AUKUS partnership vessels,” he said. The business, he noted, earns among the highest margins in U.S. defense and benefits from favorable risk-sharing arrangements with government customers.

Near-term margin pressure, longer-term recovery potential

BNP Paribas expects margins in BWXT’s government operations segment to remain under pressure in the near term. Akers noted that margins have declined by roughly 150 basis points since 2021, driven by inflation flowing through long-term contracts and a growing mix of lower-margin development work, including microreactors and space nuclear propulsion.

Margins could compress further in 2026 as the company ramps investments in uranium enrichment and depleted uranium capabilities. However, Akers sees room for improvement over the longer term as BWXT’s naval propulsion contracts, which typically run about eight years, are repriced.

While acknowledging ongoing margin pressure, Akers said earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization growth expectations for government operations appear conservative relative to broader defense spending trends, particularly given that the U.S. shipbuilding budget has increased sharply since fiscal 2024.

Commercial nuclear and medical isotopes add optionality

Beyond defense, BNP Paribas pointed to longer-term upside tied to BWXT’s exposure to commercial nuclear power and medical isotopes, though Akers emphasized that these opportunities remain optional rather than core to the near-term investment case.

BWXT’s involvement with CANDU reactors positions it as one of the few North American manufacturers capable of producing large nuclear power components, potentially supporting future large-scale nuclear projects and small modular reactors.

In medical isotopes, Akers highlighted the company’s potential exposure to growth in therapeutic radioisotopes, estimating a possible 10% to 30% share of a market that could eventually exceed $1 billion in annual revenue, subject to regulatory approvals.

Valuation supports Neutral stance

Despite those longer-term opportunities, BNP Paribas concluded that BWXT’s valuation already reflects much of the upside.

“Assuming a relative defense multiple near the midpoint of its prior range, we estimate Comm. Ops implied valuation is appropriate, near the midpoint of its nuclear peers,” Akers wrote.

Akers set a $215 price target, implying roughly 5% upside from BWXT’s recent price of about $204. The $215 price target is based on a 17.3 times ebitda multiple for government operations and a 10x sales multiple for commercial operations, according to the note.

data.title: Nabors redeems 7.5% notes due 2028, cuts Q4 net debt by about $366M
data.description:
• Nabors Industries (NYSE:NBR) said on Thursday that it has fully redeemed its 7.5% Senior Guaranteed Notes due 2028, with a face value of about $379M, at par plus accrued interest on Jan. 15, 2026.
  • As of Dec. 31, 2025, Nabors had $2.5 billion in total debt and $940 million in cash and short-term investments, resulting in net debt of $1.55 billion and the lowest net leverage since 2008.
  • The company’s net debt fell about $366M in Q4 2025, or roughly $25 per common share, bringing the total reduction since Dec. 31, 2024, to approximately $550M.
  • Shares -2.47%.

data.title: Volatility is on the menu at CAVA Group as a new battleground stock
data.description:
CAVA Group (CAVA) has become a battleground stock in the restaurant sector, according to S3 Partners.

S3 Partners Managing Director Matthew Unterman highlighted that active long interest notional is steadily declining, while short interest notional has remained resilient.

"The long-to-short ratio has compressed meaningfully, falling from >4x in early 2025 to 1.2x today, signaling a firm deterioration in long conviction. The recent steadying in active long notional, alongside persistent and increasing short exposure, reinforces the battleground setup currently in play," advised Unterman.

CAVA (CAVA) is down 38% over the last 52 weeks across a wide trading range, and positioning has become increasingly unstable. S3 Partners thinks  any fundamental or sentiment catalyst could trigger outsized moves as reduced long support meets elevated short exposure. Against that backdrop, CAVA Group (CAVA) will report fourth-quarter earnings during the later part of February. 

Seeking Alpha analyst PropNotes likes the setup on CAVA (CAVA) and assigned the restaurant stock a Strong Buy rating. "Overall, CAVA faces a number of risks, including ongoing macro headwinds, competition from peers, and a nominally high valuation. However, the company's strong execution through a difficult macro, robust unit economics, and strong growth prospects outweigh this downside," read part of the bullish assessment.
